subject: 🏃 Friday — Easy 3 mi (first run in 8 days)
html:
Today: Easy run, 3 miles on a soft surface. Sawyer Camp Trail or Bayfront paths. Pace 9:30–10:00/mi, heart rate under 140. This is your first run in 8 days — the last one (April 16, 5 mi) flagged Yellow with medial knee awareness post-run. Every gym session since has been Green, and yesterday's Hip & Core B was a real positive: Bulgarian split squat at 15 lb/hand cleared Good with zero knee complaint. That's a strong signal the chassis work is already helping.

Before you head out: Daily mobility flush first — T-spine extensions (PT), standing lunges with hip IR (PT), world's greatest stretch, 90/90, couch stretch, hip CARs, cat-cow. ~12 minutes. Non-negotiable before first run back.

Focus cue: Track knee feel by mile — note status at mile 1, 2, and 3 separately. If it moves past awareness into discomfort, cut it short immediately. No heroics on 3 miles.

Context: Week 1 of 4 in Chassis Build — 3 weeks to your PT follow-up on May 15 (93 days to race). The schedule swap worked well: Hip & Core B yesterday gave the knee an extra rest day before today's run. Tomorrow is Upper Pull, Sunday is cycling or a hike. After your run, report back with knee status by mile so I can make the call on next week's volume.
